input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule V.3.2.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.3.2.4
            TITLE: the steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.2.4: The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from
V.3.2.4 The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from 
locking up (the inversion of a four bar linkage at one of the pivots). The stops: 
a. Must prevent the wheels and tires from contacting suspension, bodywork, or Chassis 
during the track events 
b. May be placed on the uprights or on the rack

final_answer: V.3.2.4 The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from locking up (the inversion of a four bar linkage at one of the pivots). The stops: a. Must prevent the wheels and tires from contacting suspension, bodywork, or Chassis during the track events b. May be placed on the uprights or on the rack
